content.full_text:
Re
Fnrn Polrrrcnr- PRACrrcEs C ouurssroN
.128 J Street o Suite 620 o gscrurento, CA 95g14-2329
(9lO 322-5660 o Fax (9t6) lll-6966
June 1,2010
Rita D. Encinas
P. O. Box 64
Woodbridge, CA 95258
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-10-076
Dear Ms. Encinas:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding post-employment restrictions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your inquiry is general in nature, we are treating
yollr request as one for informal assistance.z
Please note that our advice is based solely on the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on
the application, if any, of other post-government employment laws such as public Contract Code
Section l04l I or on any incompatible activities restiictions your agency may impose under
Govemment Code Section 19990. We also offer no advice on the application, if any, of
Govemment Code Section 1090. We urge you to contact the Attorney General's office
regarding issues arising under the Public Contract Code and Government Code Section 1090.
Additionally, this letter should not be construed as advice on any conduct that may have already
taken place. This letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political practices Commission
("Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Ogle.sby- Og75)
I FPPC Ops. 7l; Govt. Code section g3l 14.)
QUESTTON
If you leave state service and are offered a position as a business analyst with a
consulting firm, what restrictions will be placed on your contacts with your former agency, the
Department of Transportation (.,Caltrans")?
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g 1000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the piir potitical practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through ltt997 of Title 2 of thi Calif<lrnia Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Cocle of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
I Informal assistance cloes not provide the requestor with the immunity provided'by an opinien qr f.rmal
written advice. (Regulation 18329(c).) 
t 
--- -J -"
:+l


File No. I-10-076
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
- 
The one-year ban would prohibit you from communicating with employees of Caltrans,
for one year after you leave this agency, if the communication is lor the pt,.por" of influencing
any legislative or administrative action, or influencing any discretionary act',,involving the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permii, license, gront o. contract, or the sale
or purchase of goods or property." The perrnanent ban is a lifetime ban that would prohibit you
from participating in any judicial, quasi-iudicial or other proceeding in which you participated
while a state administrative official at Caltrans or any other state agency where you previously
worked' In addition, you must abide by restriction. on a public official-who is negotiating or has
any arrangement concerning prospective employment. See discussion below.
FACTS
You work at Caltrans as a senior transportation planner. You are retiring in a couple of
months and would like to pLlrsue work as a buiiness unolyrt with two different consulting firms.
These consulting firms have contracts with Caltrans. You do not have responsibility for
approval of the consulting services provided by these firms, nor do you have any control over, or
have decision making authority over the budgets for these contracts.
Your current duties include maintaining a legacy system, which is an older computer
system' You are also a member of a team that is working io replace a legacy system, and you are
participating in design, configuration, interfaces and data-conversion witi r.g*a to this new
system.
You were on an interview panel that selected one of these firms for a consulting contract
with Caltrans. This contract was awarded in June 2008 and ends December 2010. you do not
manage this contract, nor do you culrently make any decisions about this contract. you wish to
know whether you may be employed with either of ihese consulting firms as a business analyst
once you retire from Caltrans.
A1\ALYSIS
P o s t - G ov e ntme nt al Empl oyme nt Re st ric t i ons
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types of post-governmental
employment provisions under the Act, colloquially kntwn as the'ierotuing dotr,,prohibitions.
In addition, Section 87407 prohibits certain siate and local officials from .iking, participating in
making, or using their official position to influence decisions affecting p".ronr *ith whom they
are negotiating employment, or have any alrangement conce.ning emf,t,oyment. (Section g7407;
Regulation 18747.)
One Year Ban: This ban prohibits a public official from appearing for compensation
before his or her former agency, or officer oiemployee thereof, ro.itre pu".pose or influencing


File No. I-10-076
Page No. 3
any administrative, legislative or other specified action (including contracts).
Permanent Ban: This ban prohibits a former state administrative official from advising
or representing any person' other than the State of California, for compensation in any judicial]
quasi-judicial or other proceeding in wfr]c.h the official participated *t it. in state service. (See
Sections 87 4Ol-87 402, regularion I g74 l. I ); and
Restrictions on Negotiating Prospective Employment: This prohibition restricts public
officials from negotiating or making any arrangement conceming prospective employment
(section 87 407, regulation t87 47).
The One-Year Ban
The one-year prohibition applies to employees who are designated or should be
designated in their former agency's conflicrof-interest code. As a senior transportation planner
for caltrans' you are a designated employee. Therefore, for one year after leaving Caltrans you
may not communicate with your former agency in an attempt to influence any transaction
involving legislative or administrative action3 or other specified action (including contracts).
Once you are no longer authorized to perform the duties of your job at Caltrans and you
stop performing them, the Act's one-year barand the permanent b; wiil apply. (CoterAdvice
Letter, No. I-07-089.)
Communtcattons Covered by the One_year Ban
Not all communications to a former state administrative agency employer are prohibited
by the one-year ban. The ban extends only to those communicati6ns ior the purpose of
influencing any legislative or administrative action, or influencing any discretionary act
"involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant or
contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property." (Section si+oo(axr); Regulation
18746.1(b)(5).)
These communications include, but are not limited to, conversing directly or by
telephone, corresponding by writing or e-mail, attending a meeting, and-clelivering or sending
any communication. (Regulation tg7a6.2(a).) A communication is considered to be for the
purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action "if it is made for ttre principal purpose
. 
I For purposes of section 874o6,the Act defines "administrative action" and ..legislative action,, as the
following: "'Administrative action' means rhe proposal, drafting, development, consideration, amendment,
enactment, or defeat by any state agency ofany ruie, regulation]or other action in any ratemaking proceeding or any
quasi-legislative proceeding . . .." (Section SZfjOZ(a).1 -
"'Legislative action' means the drafting, introduction, consideration, modification, enactment or defeat of
any bill, resolution' amendment, report, nomination or other matter by the Legislature or by either house or any
committee' subcommittee' joint or select committee thereof, or by a member Jr employee of the Legislature acting
in his official capacity. 'Legislative action' also means the actionof the Governor in approving or vetoing any bill.,,
(Section 82037.)


File No. I-10-076
Page No.4
of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifying, opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or
proceeding." (Regulation I 8746 .Z(a).)
Therefore, you may not appear before or communicate with Caltrans regarding the
issuance, amendment, or awarding of any contracts for a period of one year after you leave state
service.
However, you may appear before or commllnicate with Caltrans for the purpose of
administering, implementing, or fulfilling the requirements of a pre-existing cont.u"t or project,
so long as the services do not involve the issuance, amendment, iwardirg, o, revocation of a
permit, license, agreement or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods oi p.op"rty.a (Regulation
18746.1(b)(sXA); Quirtng Advice Letter, No. 4-03-272; HananAdvice Lltter, No. t-OO-ZOq.)
This is a fact-specific query. You may wish to submit more information about and
request further advice regarding a specific contract or project.
The Pennanent Ban
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial or other
proceeding in which you participated while a state administrative officiai at Caltrans or any other
state agency. (Sections 87401 and 87402.) Thus, a public official may never "switch sidei" in a
proceeding after leaving state service.
As a senior transportation planner at Caltrans, you are a state administrative officials for
purposes of the Act. Therefore you are subject to the permanent ban once you leave state
service' (Section 87400(b).) To determine if the permanent ban applies to your situation, you
would need to identify the proceedings in which you participated *iit" emiloyed by the state.
You have not provided sufficient facts or details about specific proceedings that appear to
implicate this prohibition. Nevertheless, you should be aware oi this piovision as it is
specifically applicable to any judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings you may have
participated as senior transportation planner. "Judicial, quasi-judicial oi oiher proceeding"
means any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other determination, contract, claim,
controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a
specific party or parties in any court or state administrative agency . . .." (Section g7400(c).)
a Certain c'ther types of communications are rro, restricted under the one-year ban. For instance, it is not
considered a prohibited communication under the one-year ban, if an individual: 'i l; earticipates as a panelist or
tbrmal speaker at a conference or similar public event ior educational purposes or to disseminate research and the
subject matter does not pertain to a specitic action or proceeding; (2) inends a general informational meeting,
seminar, or similar event; (3) Requests information concerning iny matter of public record: or (4) Communicates
with the press." (Regutation 19746.2(b)(l )-(4).)
5 1b) "State administrative official" means every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state
administrative agency who as part of his or her ofticial responsibilities engagei in any judicial, quasi-judicial
or other proceeding in other than a purely clerical, secretarial or ministeriil capacity.; (Section g740qb).)


File No. I-10-076
Page No. 5
Thus, for example, you indicate you were involved in Caltrans' selection of one of the
consulting firms with which you may possibly seek employment. The permanent ban would
therefore preclude you from working for that fitm on u rnoit". involving issues relevant to that
selection process, should they arise. As mentioned, however, we would need additional facts to
determine whether the permanent ban applies to your future work on other matters relating to
that contract.
Ban on Influencing Prospective Employment
Section 87407, the ban against influencing prospective employment, provides:
"No public official, shall make, participate in making, or use his or hir official position to
influence, any governmental decision directly relating to any person with whom he or she is
negotiating, or has any arrangement concerning, prospective employment.,,
The term "public official" is defined, in part, in Section 82048 as ". . . every member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency. . ." Caltrans is a state
administrative agency, as defined in Section 87400 (a), and as an employee working for Caltrans,
you are subject to this ban.
"A public official is 'negotiating' employment when he or she interviews or discusses an
offer of employment with an [potential] employer or his or her agent." (Regulation Wa7@)(l).)
The Commission has constnted the scheduling, conduct, and follow-up io ai interview as one
continuous process falling under the definition of "negotiating" employm ent. (Bonner Advice
Letter, No. I-98-287.) However, the mere act of sending a resume or application to a specific
entity has not been considered "negotiating." Similarly, entertaining informal inquiriei about
your future plans and receiving expressions of general interest in diJcussing potential
employment opportunities at some point in the future is not considered "ne-gotiating." (Id.)
"A public official has an 'arrangement' concerning prospective employment when he or
she accepts an employer's offer of employment." (Regulaiion l-g7a7@)(2)'.)
You do not describe your contacts, if any, with the consulting firm with regard to future
employment. However, please note that if you have sufficiently disJussed the proipect of your
future employment with the consulting firm to be considered part of a "negotiation,,, you would
be prohibited, pursuant to Section 87407, from making, participating in thf making, or using
your official position with Caltrans to influence any goue*m"ntal decisions that impacts th;
consulting firm. If that is the case, you would have to examine each decision you face and
ascertain if the consulting firm is involved in or affected by that decision to determine if you are
permitted to participate.
We have enclosed a copy of our fact sheet, "Revolving Door and Other post-Employment
lssues" which provides more detailed information.


File No. I-10-076
Page No.6
lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By:
Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ERjgl
